purpose: Stage 2 candidate parallel/comparable Hawaiian-English source inventory and fetch plan. Per-source: id/name, access URL, languages, alignment_type tag (matches docs/data-pipeline.md §'Stage 2 source tiers' / 'Required tagging'), rights notes, prototype-only posture, dry-run-by-default acquisition plan, expected artifact shape, verification status, and exclusions/risks. This is a planning artifact only: no raw bytes are fetched, stored, or committed by adopting this file. All real fetches still run via the scripts under scripts/ with explicit --execute flags and write under the gitignored data/ tree.
scope_notes: Private prototype / learning project. No public release of weights, tokenizer, dataset, or demo. Every source row carries prototype_only=true downstream (see docs/data-pipeline.md §'Stage 2 manifest schema').
Raw and intermediate bytes stay under the gitignored data/ tree. Only this inventory, source adapters, schemas, templates, and docs are commit-ready.
Rights status here is a hint, not a legal determination. Per-source rights review happens before any non-eval ingest beyond a smoke pull. Linus owns final data-policy review.
Verification status reflects what Frank has personally confirmed at fetch-plan time, not at row-acceptance time. A 'verified_endpoint' source can still be 'pending_rights_review'.

alignment_type_legend.parallel-verse: Verse-keyed alignment (Bible). Deterministic on a pinned edition pair.
alignment_type_legend.parallel-sentence: Sentence-aligned bitext from the source (TMX, Moses .txt, TSV, etc.).
alignment_type_legend.parallel-doc: Document-level paired material; sentence segmentation/alignment required downstream.
alignment_type_legend.comparable-aligned: Topically aligned but not pre-aligned at sentence level; LaBSE/LASER + threshold required (default 0.75 LaBSE per docs/data-pipeline.md).
alignment_type_legend.dictionary-example: Dictionary example-sentence fields; narrow register, capped, never dev/test.
alignment_type_legend.synthetic-bt: Back-translation of Stage-1 monolingual via the Stage-1-merged base (capped, never dev/test).
alignment_type_legend.synthetic-ft: Forward-translation of clean English via external MT (tighter cap, never dev/test).
alignment_type_legend.excluded: Not used in this prototype. Reason recorded in source row.

verification_status_legend.verified_endpoint: Frank confirmed the listed URL/endpoint resolves and the dataset id/config is real (e.g., HF datasets-server probe, dump index reachable).
verification_status_legend.pending_endpoint_check: URL listed but Frank has not yet confirmed the exact dataset id / config / file path.
verification_status_legend.pending_rights_review: Endpoint may be real but rights posture for our use is not settled. No fetch beyond a tiny smoke pull until Linus signs off.
verification_status_legend.excluded_pending_verification: Source is explicitly NOT approved. Listed only so future agents do not silently re-add it. Do not fetch.

rights_status_legend.public_domain_candidate: Source itself appears to be in the public domain (e.g., pre-1925 US imprints); per-document confirmation still required.
rights_status_legend.open_license_candidate: Source advertises an open license (e.g., CC BY-SA 4.0, CC BY 2.0 FR); flow-down conditions must be respected.
rights_status_legend.rights_review_required: Mixed or unclear rights; manual per-document/per-row review before any non-eval use.
rights_status_legend.eval_only: Restricted to evaluation: hash into eval_hashes.jsonl before anything else, never train.
rights_status_legend.unknown_review_required: Rights posture not yet established; treat as restricted until reviewed.

global_acquisition_defaults.execute_flag_required: All fetcher scripts default to dry-run; an explicit --execute flag is required to write bytes. No fetcher is invoked by reading or merging this inventory.
global_acquisition_defaults.stage2_storage_root: data/stage2/  (gitignored; Linus owns the manifest writer)
global_acquisition_defaults.evals_storage_root: data/evals/  (gitignored; eval-only sources land here and are hashed into data/evals/eval_hashes.jsonl BEFORE any other ingest)
global_acquisition_defaults.provenance_record_shape: Reuse the ProvenanceRecord shape used by scripts/205_fetch_fineweb2_haw_raw.py (source_url, fetch_timestamp_utc, http_status, content_length, raw_sha256, raw_storage_path, license_observed, tos_or_license_url, tos_snapshot_path, source_specific_ids, edition_or_version, fetcher_user_agent, fetcher_tool_and_version).
tos_snapshot_required_for: any source whose license_observed is per-page or per-row
global_acquisition_defaults.rate_limits: Polite by default: ≤1 req/sec per host unless the host publishes a higher allowance. HF datasets-server is known-rate-limit-sensitive; sustained pulls use --rate-limit-seconds 3.0 (see frank history 2026-04-29 FineWeb-2 fetch).
global_excluded_categories: Hard-escalate cultural categories (mele, oli, pule, moʻolelo from named tradition-bearers, moʻokūʻauhau) per docs/data-pipeline.md §52 — even for prototype use, until a cultural-review owner exists.
Auto-MT outputs not flagged synthetic=true on either side.
Social-media / forum bilingual posts without explicit per-poster permission.
LLM-generated synthetic Hawaiian dialogues not grounded in a real source pair.
Anything where one side is auto-MT and the pair is not flagged synthetic-bt or synthetic-ft.

sources.name: Baibala Hemolele — pinned Hawaiian edition (verse-aligned)
sources.license_observed: Site ToS governs the live presentation. Pre-1925 imprint editions (e.g., 1868 Andrews/Bingham) are the public-domain candidate path; Linus must approve which edition we pin.
prototype_notes: Largest reliable parallel pool for Hawaiian. Verse-level only; chapter-level alignment is unsafe across editions.
Cap ≤30% of parallel-train tokens; 0% of dev/test (per docs/data-pipeline.md §'Stage 2 source tiers' Tier A).
Pin one Hawaiian edition + one public-domain English edition; record edition_or_version per row.
alignment_method='verse-id' (deterministic); no LaBSE needed.

steps: Confirm pinned edition with Linus (rights ruling on edition year + provenance).
Snapshot site ToS to data/raw/bible-haw-baibala-pinned-edition/<YYYYMMDD>/tos_snapshot.html before any fetch.
Dry-run: enumerate book/chapter URLs from the pinned edition without fetching verse bodies.
On --execute: fetch chapter pages, persist raw HTML + provenance record, parse to verse-keyed records (book, chapter, verse, edition_year).

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: book, chapter, verse, text_haw, edition_or_version, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Bible-token-share blowing past the 30% parallel-train cap — sample down rather than fetching more Bible.
Edition-mismatch contamination across reprints — cluster-isolated dedup required.

sources.name: English Bible — public-domain anchor (KJV / ASV via eBible.org)
sources.license_observed: KJV / ASV verse text is public domain in the US; eBible packaging metadata may carry its own license — record both.
prototype_notes: Used only as the English-side anchor for the pinned Hawaiian edition.
USFM parsing required to recover verse-keyed records (book, chapter, verse).
Rights are cleanest of all Stage-2 candidates; safe to fetch in full once edition is pinned.

steps: Confirm edition (KJV vs ASV) with Linus; lock to the same anchor across the Stage-2 prototype.
Dry-run: HEAD request the USFM zip, record content_length and ETag.
On --execute: download zip, persist raw bytes + provenance record, extract per-book USFM.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: book, chapter, verse, text_eng, edition_or_version, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: USFM-to-plain-text extraction must drop section headers, footnote anchors, and editorial brackets to avoid leaking non-translation tokens into target_text.

sources.name: archive.org — Baibala Hemolele scanned editions (pre-1925)
sources.license_observed: Pre-1925 imprints are public_domain_candidate in the US; confirm imprint year per item.
prototype_notes: Alternative / supplement to baibala.org for pinning a specific historical edition (e.g., 1868 Andrews/Bingham named in docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2).
OCR quality varies wildly per scan; OCR confidence must be persisted per page so downstream filters can drop low-confidence verses.
Use the existing internetarchive bucket entry in data-sources/hawaiian-data-sources.json (do not duplicate adapters).

acquisition_plan.rate_limit: Per IA client defaults; no aggressive parallelism.
steps: Dry-run: list candidate items, record imprint year and ocr_quality_indicator.
Pick one item per chosen edition.
On --execute: pull OCR text + page images metadata; persist per-page raw + per-verse extraction.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: book, chapter, verse, text_haw, edition_or_version, publication_year, ia_item_id, ocr_quality_indicator, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: OCR errors silently corrupting verse text — gate on ocr_quality_indicator and require human spot-check on a sample.
Multiple printings of the same edition causing near-duplicate verses across items — cluster-isolated dedup required.

sources.name: Tatoeba — Hawaiian sentences and haw↔eng links
sources.license_observed: CC BY 2.0 FR (per Tatoeba site). Per-sentence contributor + license_per_sentence must be preserved for attribution flow-down.
prototype_notes: Hundreds (low-thousands) of pairs at most; small but clean signal.
Hash all pairs into eval_hashes.jsonl decision space before splitting train vs dev (per docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2 contamination & eval guards).
alignment_method='tatoeba-link-id' (deterministic via the haw-eng_links.tsv table).

steps: Dry-run: HEAD all three .tsv.bz2 endpoints, record content_length.
On --execute: download all three, persist raw bytes + provenance.
Decompress and join sentences ↔ links to materialize haw↔eng pairs with tatoeba_sentence_id and contributor preserved.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: tatoeba_sentence_id_haw, tatoeba_sentence_id_eng, text_haw, text_eng, contributor_haw, contributor_eng, license_per_sentence, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Volume too small to support both train and dev — likely train+held-out only.
Per-sentence attribution must survive into the final manifest; license flow-down obligation.

sources.name: OPUS — haw-containing parallel subsets (Tatoeba, QED, Ubuntu, GNOME, KDE)
sources.license_observed: Per-corpus (varies). Software-l10n sets are typically permissive but heterogeneous; record license per corpus version.
prototype_notes: Software-l10n sets (Ubuntu/GNOME/KDE) are domain-skewed; tag register='software-l10n' and cap.
Confirm exact version IDs at fetch time; OPUS releases shift.
JW300 is intentionally absent from this list — see deferred_or_excluded.jw300_haw.

steps: Dry-run: HEAD each .zip, record content_length and version.
On --execute: download per-corpus zip, persist raw bytes + provenance, tag register per corpus.
Parse Moses-format pairs; preserve opus_corpus, opus_version, pair_direction.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: opus_corpus, opus_version, pair_direction, text_en, text_haw, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256, register

exclusions_or_risks: Domain skew toward software UI strings — cap by register and never let it dominate parallel-train tokens.
Per-corpus license heterogeneity — per-corpus rights snapshot before --execute.
Internal Tatoeba subset is duplicate-prone with the upstream Tatoeba source — cluster-isolated dedup.

sources.name: NLLB Seed / NLLB-MD mined haw↔eng (Hugging Face mirror)
sources.license_observed: Per dataset card; mined data is noisy and rights-mixed. Re-derive provenance from origin URLs, not the HF mirror.
prototype_notes: Mined parallel — train signal only, NEVER dev/test (per docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2 Tier A).
Confirm hawn_Latn / haw_Latn coverage and license before pulling; treat as last-resort, not P0.
If retained, alignment_score must be persisted; below-threshold rows are alignment_review_required=true.

acquisition_plan.auth_required: HF token may be required for full dataset pulls; use datasets-server /rows for smoke probes (stdlib-only path).
steps: Dry-run: hit datasets-server /rows with length=1 for the relevant config to confirm haw coverage and schema.
Defer full pull until Linus rules on mined-data rights posture and Rusty rules on quality threshold.
On --execute (post-rulings): pull rows with origin URL preserved per row; never fetch via mirror without origin retention.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: dataset_revision, config_name, split, row_id, text_en, text_haw, origin_url_en, origin_url_haw, alignment_score, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Mined-quality noise: false-positive alignment, code-mixed, MT contamination.
Origin-URL rights mixed; per-row provenance is the floor.
NEVER promote to dev/test under any circumstance.

sources.name: BibleNLP Corpus — haw subset
sources.license_observed: Per dataset card; varies per source translation. Cross-check against the pinned baibala.org / archive.org edition rather than substituting it.
prototype_notes: Useful as a CROSS-CHECK against our pinned Hawaiian Bible edition and as a verse-ID sanity test for the alignment adapter — not a replacement for the pinned edition.
Confirm exactly which Hawaiian translation the haw config wraps before treating it as our anchor; it may not be the 1868 edition the docs name.

steps: Dry-run: probe datasets-server /rows for config(s) containing haw.
Inspect a sample of <=10 rows to identify which translation is wrapped.
Defer any bulk pull until edition identity matches Linus's pinned edition or is explicitly accepted as a cross-check.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: dataset_revision, config_name, split, row_id, book, chapter, verse, text_haw, text_eng?, edition_or_version, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Edition opacity: if the wrapped translation is unidentified or modern-rights-encumbered, treat as excluded for training; eval-only at most.
Verse-coverage holes (some Hawaiian translations omit deuterocanonicals or have alternate book divisions) — surface coverage in the smoke probe.

sources.name: Global-PIQA Parallel — haw_Latn slice
sources.license_observed: Per dataset card; treat as eval-only regardless of upstream license. Hash before any other ingest.
prototype_notes: Held-out non-Bible eval anchor candidate (per docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2 immediate next steps and frank's W1 Stage-0 shortlist).
Hash all selected rows into data/evals/eval_hashes.jsonl BEFORE any Stage-1 / Stage-2 train ingest re-runs.
NEVER train on this source.
Lands under the evals division (data/evals/global_piqa_haw/), not stage2/.

acquisition_plan.rate_limit: HF: --rate-limit-seconds 3.0 sustained for /rows; resolve/main is a single static file.
steps: Dry-run: HEAD parallel_haw_latn.tsv, record content_length and ETag.
On --execute: download TSV, persist raw bytes + provenance, hash every row into data/evals/eval_hashes.jsonl.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: TSV passthrough; per-row sha256 added; rows hashed into data/evals/eval_hashes.jsonl

exclusions_or_risks: Eval contamination if any row leaks into Stage-1 or Stage-2 train (the eval_hashes.jsonl ledger is the backstop).
Coverage may be small; do not over-rely as the sole eval anchor — pair with held-out Tatoeba and Bible-anchored verse pairs.

sources.name: Taxi1500 — haw_Latn diagnostic
sources.license_observed: Per repo / dataset card; Bible-derived classification eval. Treat as eval-only.
prototype_notes: Diagnostic only — its Bible/classification shape biases toward religious register, so it is not a primary anchor.
Useful as a sanity-check signal alongside global-piqa-parallel and held-out Tatoeba.
Hash any selected rows into eval_hashes.jsonl before anything else.

steps: Dry-run: identify the haw_Latn split file path in the repo, record commit SHA.
On --execute: pin to a commit SHA, fetch the haw_Latn split, persist raw bytes + provenance, hash rows.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: TSV/JSONL passthrough with classification labels preserved; rows hashed into data/evals/eval_hashes.jsonl

exclusions_or_risks: Register skew (Bible-derived) — do not let it dominate eval reporting.
Classification framing differs from translation framing — keep diagnostic-only, not a translation-quality anchor.

sources.name: Weblate localization — en↔haw translation memories
sources.license_observed: Per project on Weblate; many projects are GPL/BSD/Apache/MIT/CC-BY-SA but some are proprietary. Per-project license capture is mandatory.
prototype_notes: Software-l10n register; tag register='software-l10n' and cap (same posture as OPUS Ubuntu/GNOME/KDE).
Per-project license heterogeneity: capture license per project before merging into a single manifest.
Volume per project is small but the sum across haw projects can exceed Tatoeba.

acquisition_plan.auth_required: Some projects require an API token; public projects expose TM downloads without auth.
steps: Dry-run: enumerate haw projects via the Weblate REST API; record per-project license.
Filter to permissive-license projects only.
On --execute: pull per-project translation memory (TMX or CSV); persist raw + provenance per project.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: project_slug, project_license, text_en, text_haw, tm_unit_id, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Mixed per-project licenses — easy to accidentally fold a non-permissive project in; the per-project filter is a hard gate.
UI-string register skew; cap as with OPUS l10n subsets.

sources.name: Hawaiian ↔ English Wikipedia (interlanguage links, comparable docs)
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0 + GFDL (per-revision; Wikimedia Terms apply).
prototype_notes: Topically aligned, NOT sentence-aligned. LaBSE/LASER + similarity threshold (default 0.75 LaBSE per docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2) required downstream.
Expect <5k usable pairs after threshold + length-ratio + LID filters.
Cleanest licensing posture among comparable sources.

acquisition_plan.rate_limit: MediaWiki API: ≤1 req/sec; use User-Agent identifying this prototype.
steps: Dry-run: enumerate haw-page titles from the existing hawwiki dump (already fetched per Stage-1 plan), then query langlinks API for en counterparts without fetching article bodies.
On --execute: fetch en-side article extracts for matched titles, persist raw + provenance.
Sentence-segment per side; LaBSE-align downstream (Linus owns the alignment pass).

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: haw_title, haw_revision_id, en_title, en_revision_id, text_haw, text_eng, source_url_haw, source_url_eng, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Comparable-not-parallel: false-friend pairs at chapter/section level — alignment score gate is mandatory.

Stage-1 contamination: the Hawaiian side may overlap the Stage-1 hawwiki monolingual corpus; cluster-isolated dedup against Stage-1 hashes is required (see crosslink_stage1_overlap field in stage2_manifest).

sources.name: Hawaiian ↔ English Wikisource (per-text comparable)
sources.license_observed: CC BY-SA 4.0 wrapper over public-domain source texts; per-text provenance in the page metadata.
prototype_notes: Per-text manual check; volume small.
Where a Hawaiian text has an explicit English Wikisource counterpart, alignment_method may upgrade to 'parallel-doc' before sentence segmentation.
Useful for register diversity beyond Bible + Wikipedia + Tatoeba.

steps: Dry-run: enumerate haw.wikisource pages and look for explicit en counterparts.
On --execute: fetch matched pairs with per-revision IDs, persist raw + provenance, sentence-segment per side.

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: haw_page_title, haw_revision_id, en_page_title?, en_revision_id?, text_haw, text_eng?, source_url_haw, source_url_eng?, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Many Hawaiian Wikisource pages have no English counterpart — yield is small.
Hard-escalate cultural categories must be filtered out at enumeration time (mele, oli, pule, moʻolelo from named tradition-bearers, moʻokūʻauhau).

sources.name: Pukui-Elbert / Andrews dictionary example sentences
sources.license_observed: Per-item; pre-1925 imprints (Andrews 1865) are public_domain_candidate. Modern Pukui-Elbert printings are NOT public domain.
prototype_notes: ONLY example-sentence fields are pair candidates. Headword-only entries are NOT training pairs.
Tag register='dictionary-example', cap, exclude from dev/test (per docs/data-pipeline.md Tier C).
Pin a public-domain edition (e.g., Andrews 1865) for prototype use; defer modern Pukui-Elbert until Linus rules.

steps: Dry-run: list candidate IA items, record imprint year, OCR quality.
Pick one PD item.
On --execute: pull OCR text, extract entries, isolate example-sentence fields per entry.
do_not_invoke_until: edition pinned by Linus (PD vs modern)

expected_artifact_shape.extracted_records: JSONL with fields: dictionary_edition, headword, example_haw, example_eng, page, source_url, fetch_date, raw_sha256

exclusions_or_risks: Headword-only rows polluting the manifest if the extractor is sloppy — example-field extraction must be schema-checked.
Modern Pukui-Elbert is rights-encumbered; do not fetch the modern edition without explicit rights ruling.
Register skew: dictionary-example ≠ natural prose.

deferred_or_excluded.name: JW300 — haw subset
deferred_or_excluded.reason: Explicitly excluded per docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2 Tier E ('Excluded for Stage 2 prototype') unless ToS is re-verified. JW300 is treated as UNVERIFIED for this prototype: no fetch, no smoke pull, no inclusion in any merged manifest. Listed here only so future agents do not silently re-add it. If Linus performs a fresh ToS verification and the posture changes, this row must be moved to `sources` with a verified_endpoint status, a license_observed field reflecting the confirmed posture, and the exact ToS snapshot path captured at verification time. Until then: do not fetch.

deferred_or_excluded.name: General web crawls / Common Crawl Hawaiian slices
deferred_or_excluded.reason: Undercurated scrapes are out of scope per Linus's posture. Stage 1 already pulls from FineWeb-2 haw_Latn with per-row URL provenance; arbitrary CC slices are not a Stage 2 parallel candidate.

deferred_or_excluded.name: Mele, oli, pule, moʻolelo from named tradition-bearers, moʻokūʻauhau
deferred_or_excluded.reason: Excluded per docs/data-pipeline.md §52 until a cultural-review owner exists, even for prototype use. This applies to any Stage-2 source that would surface these categories incidentally (e.g., Wikisource enumeration must filter them at discovery time, not after fetch).

deferred_or_excluded.name: Social-media / forum bilingual posts
deferred_or_excluded.reason: No explicit per-poster permission; ToS rarely permits redistribution. Out of scope for this prototype.

deferred_or_excluded.name: LLM-generated 'synthetic Hawaiian dialogues' not grounded in a real source pair
deferred_or_excluded.reason: Excluded per docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2 Tier E. Synthetic-bt / synthetic-ft remain allowed under cap, but ungrounded synthesis is out.

open_questions.question: Which Hawaiian Bible edition do we pin (e.g., 1868 Andrews/Bingham via baibala.org vs an archive.org pre-1925 imprint), and which English PD edition (KJV vs ASV)? Pinning unblocks bible-haw-baibala-pinned-edition, bible-eng-pd-anchor, bible-haw-archive-org-pre1925, and the verse-ID adapter.
Mined-data rights posture for nllb-mined-haw-eng — accept origin-URL provenance per row at prototype scope, or require an allow/deny list before any pull?
Pukui-Elbert vs Andrews dictionary edition for pukui-elbert-andrews-examples — pinning a PD edition (Andrews 1865) is the safe default; confirm before any --execute.

LaBSE/LASER threshold for comparable-aligned sources (wiki-haw-en-langlinks, wikisource-haw-en-comparable). Default in docs is 0.75 LaBSE; should we tune on Bible distribution before threshold-gating Wikipedia?

Endpoint-verification sweep for the four `pending_endpoint_check` sources (opus-haw-subsets, biblenlp-haw, global-piqa-parallel-haw, taxi1500-haw, wikisource-haw-en-comparable, weblate-en-haw, bible-haw-archive-org-pre1925, pukui-elbert-andrews-examples) — owned by Frank as a follow-up; does not block this inventory landing.

consistency_notes: alignment_type values match docs/data-pipeline.md §'Required tagging — parallel vs comparable vs synthetic'.

stage2 routing in data-sources/hawaiian-data-sources.json is preserved; this file is the per-source fetch-plan layer that file did not carry. No source rows in hawaiian-data-sources.json are removed or contradicted by this artifact.

Bible token-share cap (≤30% of parallel-train tokens), 'never train on FLORES / global-piqa / Taxi1500', and 'never dev/test on mined / synthetic' policies all flow from docs/data-pipeline.md §Stage 2.
dataset divisions follow .squad/decisions.md §'Final Dataset Taxonomy' — eval-only sources land under data/evals/, all other Stage-2 sources land under data/stage2/, raw bytes under data/raw/.
